title: I Spent $3,000 in My First Month
subtitle: Real cost breakdown. Why running Opus 24/7 is expensive. The 5 habits that brought it down to $200.

sections.heading: The Bill That Made Me Sick
sections.content: First month: $3,000+. Opus on Claude API running 24/7. Every "hi, you there?" was a billable call.

sections.heading: How AI Costs Actually Work
sections.content: Input tokens + output tokens = cost. Opus is 10-15x more expensive than Sonnet. Context window size matters.

sections.heading: The 5 Cost Traps
sections.content: Running Opus for simple tasks. Not monitoring usage. Unnecessary context. Repeated failed calls. Testing in production.

sections.heading: The 5 Habits That Fixed It
sections.content: Use the right model for the task. Monitor costs daily. Clear context when done. Cache when possible. Set spending limits.

sections.heading: The Real Numbers
sections.content: Month 1: $3,000. Month 2: ~$800 (started monitoring). Now: ~$200/month with MiniMax for Vivienne.

sections.heading: The "$100/Month" Myth
sections.content: You can run an agent for $100/month — but not on Opus, and not without discipline.

sections.heading: Free and Cheap Options
sections.content: Claude Pro ($20/month), Gemini free tier, MiniMax flat rate ($50/month), local models via Ollama.

sections.heading: The One Rule
sections.content: Know what you're spending before you spend it. Check your dashboard daily until it becomes habit.

key_takeaways: Opus costs 10-15x more than Sonnet — match the model to the task complexity
The 5 habits that cut costs from $3,000 to $200/month: right model, monitor daily, clear context, cache, set limits
Check your spending dashboard daily until it becomes habit
